title: 🟦 3x3 Square Matrix
description: Read all values in spiral order from a standard square matrix.

title: 🧱 3x4 Rectangular Matrix
description: Traverse a wider matrix in spiral order from the outer ring to the center.

title: 📏 4x3 Tall Matrix
description: Traverse a taller rectangular matrix while preserving spiral order.

title: 🎯 Single Element
description: Return the only element when the matrix contains one cell.

title: ➡️ Single Row
description: Read left to right when the matrix has only one row.

title: ⬇️ Single Column
description: Read top to bottom when the matrix has only one column.

title: 🔄 2x2 Matrix
description: Complete one simple spiral loop around a small square matrix.

title: 🧩 2x3 Matrix
description: Traverse a compact rectangular matrix with one inner turn.

title: 🪜 3x2 Matrix
description: Traverse a narrow matrix in spiral order from top edge to inner cells.

title: 🌪️ 4x4 Layered Matrix
description: Visit outer and inner layers of a larger square matrix in spiral order.

title: 🧮 Mixed Positive And Negative Values
description: Preserve spiral order even when the matrix contains negative numbers and zero.

title: 🔢 Repeated Values
description: Return all elements in spiral order even when values repeat.

title: 🎯 5x5 Center Finish
description: Traverse multiple layers and end at a single center element in an odd-sized square matrix.

title: 🧊 4x5 Multi-Layer Rectangle
description: Traverse a rectangular matrix with more than one spiral layer and no single center cell.

title: 📐 1x10 Thin Matrix
description: Handle a very wide matrix that should be read straight across once.

title: 🗼 10x1 Tall Matrix
description: Handle a very tall matrix that should be read straight down once.

title: 🫧 All Values Same
description: Preserve traversal order correctly even when every cell has the same value.

title: ⚠️ Boundary Turn Check
description: Exercise tight boundary updates in a matrix shape that can reveal duplicate-visit mistakes.

title: 🚀 10x10 Stress Case
description: Traverse a larger matrix with many layers to simulate a near-maximum constraint scenario.
